issue.author: cardigan1008
issue.body:
Instsimplify can fold:

```llvm
%cmp = icmp eq <2 x ptr> %a, %b
%sel = select <2 x i1> %cmp, <2 x ptr> %a, %b
```

to `ret <2 x ptr> %b`, which is not always valid for pointers. Even if `%a[0]` and `%b[0]` have the same address , they might have different provenance, so selecting `%a[0]` is not equivalent to returning `%b[0]`.

Alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/JxDjAx

Exposed from https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/1af627b592dd15bbe58136f902ced46251fc344d, cc @dtcxzyw

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: In https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/393783fff844d8919a090f74e641d78c50bcc7f4/llvm/lib/Analysis/Loads.cpp#L845-L873 we should replace the pointer checks with pointer or vector of pointer checks.

issue.title: [InstSimplify] Folds vector pointer `select (icmp eq <N x ptr>)` to operand and loses provenance

tests.test_body:
define void @select_same_obj(i1 %c, ptr %p, i64 %x) {
entry:
  %p2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %p, i64 %x
  %p3 = select i1 %c, ptr %p, ptr %p2
  %cmp = icmp eq ptr %p, %p3
  br i1 %cmp, label %if, label %exit

if:                                               ; preds = %entry
  call void @use_ptr(ptr %p)
  call void @use_ptr(ptr %p3)
  ret void

exit:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret void
}

declare void @use_ptr(ptr)

define ptr @ptr_eq_replace_same_underlying_object(ptr %st, i64 %i, i64 %j) {
  %a = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %st, i64 %i
  %b = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %st, i64 %j
  %cmp = icmp eq ptr %a, %b
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, ptr %a, ptr %b
  ret ptr %sel
}
